title: The sort order that vanished
hook: Your report worked for fifteen years. On HANA it returns the same rows in the wrong order.
summary: Classic ABAP quietly relied on the database returning rows in a predictable sequence. HANA gives no such guarantee. Any SELECT without an explicit ORDER BY, and any control-break logic that assumed pre-sorted records, can now produce wrong results without throwing a single error.
search: ABAP SELECT ORDER BY HANA result set order

title: The tables that aren't there anymore
hook: Half the tables your custom code reads don't exist in S/4HANA.
summary: Secondary index tables were replaced by CDS compatibility views over the Universal Journal, KONV became PRCD_ELEMENTS, and cluster and pooled tables were converted to transparent. Compatibility views cover many reads but not direct writes or native SQL.
search: S/4HANA compatibility views simplification list

title: Code pushdown, or why your loop is the problem
hook: You moved to the fastest database on earth and your report got slower.
summary: The ECC habit was to pull data up into ABAP and process it in loops. HANA rewards the opposite: do the work in the database with CDS views and AMDP, and return a result rather than a dataset. This is where the actual migration performance case lives.
search: ABAP code pushdown CDS AMDP

title: When MATNR grew to 40 characters
hook: One field got longer and three hundred programs broke.
summary: Material number extended from 18 to 40 characters. Anything with a hardcoded length, an offset operation, a fixed-width structure, a flat-file interface, or a screen field sized for 18 characters becomes a candidate for failure.
search: material number field length extension 40 characters

title: ATC, readiness checks, and the honest custom-code inventory
hook: Before you argue about the migration date, run this.
summary: ATC with S/4HANA readiness checks, the simplification item catalog, and remote analysis against a target release tell you what your custom code will actually cost. Most teams either skip it or can't read the output. Triaging noise from genuine blockers is the skill.
search: ABAP Test Cockpit S/4HANA readiness custom code migration

title: The Universal Journal, explained without the marketing
hook: FI and CO stopped being separate. Here's what that actually means at month end.
summary: ACDOCA merges general ledger, controlling, asset accounting and material ledger line items into one table. No reconciliation between FI and CO because there is nothing to reconcile. It also invalidates a lot of reporting and custom extracts built over decades.
search: Universal Journal ACDOCA S/4HANA Finance

title: Ledgers, accounting principles, and the mapping that breaks postings
hook: The config was ninety-five percent complete. The missing five percent stopped every treasury posting.
summary: S/4HANA enforces the universal journal far more strictly than classic G/L. If an accounting principle isn't mapped to a ledger for a company code, postings that used to slide through simply fail. A one-line configuration gap presenting as a system-wide outage.
search: ledger accounting principle assignment company code

title: Business Partner: the conversion nobody scoped properly
hook: Customer and vendor masters are now one object, and it's not optional.
summary: Customer/Vendor Integration and the mandatory move to Business Partner is consistently underestimated. Data quality problems that were survivable for twenty years become hard blockers: duplicates, incomplete records, inconsistent number ranges, all surfacing at once.
search: Business Partner customer vendor integration conversion

title: New Asset Accounting and mandatory Material Ledger
hook: Two modules became mandatory. Did anyone tell your finance team?
summary: New Asset Accounting is required in S/4HANA with real consequences for parallel valuation and depreciation areas. Material Ledger is likewise mandatory, changing inventory valuation and raising actual costing questions many organisations never had to answer.
search: New Asset Accounting Material Ledger S/4HANA

title: Everything that quietly became obsolete
hook: SD credit management, classic output, and four other things that don't exist anymore.
summary: A tour of the deprecations that catch teams out: SD credit management replaced by FSCM Credit Management, classic NAST and SAPscript output giving way to BRF+ driven output, foreign trade moving to GTS, and other simplification items. Each is small; collectively they're a project.
search: S/4HANA simplification item list

title: Brownfield, greenfield, or selective — and what each costs you afterwards
hook: Choose the conversion path wrong and you'll support that decision for a decade.
summary: System conversion, new implementation, or selective data transition. Most comparisons stop at project cost. The more useful angle is what each path does to support afterwards: how much history you carry, how much custom baggage survives, how clean the starting point is.
search: system conversion new implementation selective data transition

title: Sizing for in-memory: why your old rules don't apply
hook: On HANA, the data you never deleted is now the data you're paying to keep in memory.
summary: Row store to column store, disk to in-memory. Sizing works differently and data volume translates far more directly into cost, which is why archiving and housekeeping before conversion has a hard financial payback.
search: HANA sizing data volume management archiving

title: The conversion run itself — SUM, DMO, and downtime
hook: What actually happens during the weekend everyone's afraid of.
summary: Software Update Manager with the Database Migration Option in plain language: the phases, where things typically go wrong, how downtime-optimised approaches work, and what a realistic cutover weekend looks like. Most available material is vendor slides or dense documentation.
search: Software Update Manager database migration option downtime

title: Fiori changed who calls you and why
hook: Your users stopped asking for transaction codes. Now they ask why a tile is missing.
summary: The launchpad, catalogs, groups, spaces and pages, and the shift from PFCG-centric thinking to business catalogs and roles. This changes the daily support workload: different tickets, different diagnostics, different skills.
search: Fiori launchpad catalog group spaces pages role

title: Continuous change: from annual upgrades to a cadence that never stops
hook: You used to upgrade every three years. Now something changes every quarter, forever.
summary: Cloud-era release cadence and the maintenance horizons, and what continuous change does to regression testing, release monitoring and team planning. Test automation and release management stop being nice-to-haves and become core competencies.
search: SAP release and maintenance strategy

title: Why your ABAP report takes ten minutes
hook: SELECT SINGLE inside a LOOP. A thousand rows means a thousand round trips.
summary: The classic ABAP performance sin, why it scales so badly, and the fix: pull the data once before the loop and read from an internal table inside it. Includes the FOR ALL ENTRIES gotchas worth mentioning before the comments do.
search: ABAP SELECT inside LOOP performance FOR ALL ENTRIES

title: What Joule actually does for developers
hook: SAP has an AI that writes ABAP now. Here's where it genuinely helps.
summary: Joule for Developers inside ADT and VS Code: natural-language CDS generation, unit test generation, code explanation, and real-time flagging of deprecated APIs and Clean Core violations. Assistant, not autopilot.
search: Joule for developers ABAP

title: Is ABAP dying? An honest answer
hook: No. But the comfortable version of the job is ending.
summary: What's genuinely at risk, what gets more valuable, and why. The shift from writing code to judging it, the Clean Core reckoning, and where durable value concentrates for people willing to move up the stack.
search: ABAP Cloud clean core strategy

title: Classic SAP workflow is being replaced
hook: Your old workflow models don't automatically convert. You rebuild them.
summary: Workflow Management gave way to SAP Build Process Automation, and the direction of travel is low-code authoring and AI agents that decide rather than just route. Near term it's a rebuild wave and a governance problem.
search: SAP Build Process Automation workflow migration

title: Claude vs Joule: which AI for which task
hook: The question isn't which is better. It's which one for what.
summary: SAP-native AI has system depth and stays inside your tenant; a general model has breadth for design, cross-stack work and communication. Think wide, build deep — and never paste proprietary code or regulated data into an external tool.
search: Joule data privacy tenant boundary

title: PI/PO has an end date. What that means for your career
hook: You built a career on a platform with a maintenance horizon. Nobody's talking to you about it.
summary: Process Integration and Process Orchestration are on a defined maintenance path, with Integration Suite on BTP as the successor. This is the honest version: what transfers, what doesn't, roughly how long you have, and why integration people are better positioned than they think.
verify: Confirm current maintenance and extended-maintenance dates for Process Orchestration against SAP's published maintenance strategy before filming — these have been revised more than once.
search: Process Orchestration maintenance Integration Suite

title: What actually migrates from PI to Cloud Integration
hook: Your interfaces don't lift and shift. Some convert, some get rebuilt, and you need to know which is which.
summary: Mapping the old world onto the new: what the migration assessment tooling can convert, what has to be rebuilt as an iFlow by hand, and how to triage hundreds of interfaces into keep, rebuild, and retire before anyone commits to a date.
verify: Check which conversion paths the current Integration Suite migration tooling supports — coverage has expanded over time.
search: PI PO Cloud Integration migration assessment

title: From message mapping to Groovy
hook: Graphical mapping was your bread and butter. Cloud Integration wants a script.
summary: The practical skills transition: where graphical mapping still applies, where scripting takes over, and how a PI mapping specialist becomes productive in the new tooling without pretending it's the same job.
search: Cloud Integration message mapping Groovy script

title: Connectivity when half your landscape is cloud
hook: The adapter was the easy part. Now there's a tunnel, a certificate, and a firewall team.
summary: On-premise adapters gave way to cloud connectivity patterns — secure tunnels back to on-prem systems, certificate management, and network topology decisions that used to belong to someone else. Where the new complexity actually lives.
search: Cloud Connector connectivity Integration Suite

title: Point-to-point, ESB, or API-led — and who's paying
hook: Every integration you build is a liability someone maintains for ten years.
summary: The architectural choice framed as a business decision: what point-to-point actually costs at scale, when a broker earns its keep, and how API-led thinking changes what you agree to build in the first place.
search: SAP Integration Suite API management strategy

title: Monitoring moved, and so did your job
hook: You knew exactly where to look when a message failed. That muscle memory is now worth less.
summary: Message monitoring, error handling and reprocessing in the new world: where failures surface, what alerting looks like, and how the support routine changes when integration runs on a platform you don't administer.
search: Cloud Integration monitoring message processing error

title: Classic workflow, Flexible Workflow, and knowing which you're in
hook: Two workflow engines, one system, and completely different skills.
summary: S/4HANA runs Flexible Workflow for many standard processes while classic Business Workflow still exists underneath. Knowing which one a given process uses — and which one you should build in — is the first question, and plenty of teams get it wrong.
search: Flexible Workflow versus classic Business Workflow

title: When workflow stopped being development
hook: You used to build approval flows in a graphical builder. Now a functional consultant configures a scenario.
summary: Flexible Workflow moves common approval processes from developer-built definitions to configured scenarios with condition-based steps. What that means if workflow development was your niche, and where genuine development is still required.
search: Flexible Workflow scenario configuration

title: Agent determination: the thing that always breaks
hook: The workflow works. It just went to the wrong person, or nobody at all.
summary: Why agent determination causes more production incidents than any other part of workflow, how rules and org structure interact, and the diagnostic sequence that finds the cause quickly. Genuinely useful, rarely explained well.
search: workflow agent determination responsible rule

title: Stuck work items and the support playbook
hook: Someone left the company and now three hundred approvals are in limbo.
summary: The practical support routine for workflow: finding stuck and errored work items, understanding why they stopped, forwarding or restarting safely, and the housekeeping that stops the same thing happening every quarter.
search: workflow work item error restart monitoring

title: Workflow is going agentic. Who signs off then?
hook: An agent decided and acted. Now explain that to your auditor.
summary: Process automation is moving from rules that route to agents that decide. The technology question is easy; the governance one isn't. Segregation of duties, audit trails, and where a human still has to stay in the loop — especially in regulated environments.
search: SAP Build Process Automation agents governance
